Italian geopolitical analyst Giulio Chinappi, in an interview with Mehr News Agency, has characterized the U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as "coercive economic warfare" and a violation of international law absent a UN Security Council mandate. He warned that President Trump's rhetoric of civilizational annihilation and the collapse of Islamabad talks signal a premeditated strategy of capitulation rather than genuine diplomacy, sending a damaging message to the Global South about the selective enforcement of international law. 🔗

Iran’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the United Nations in New York, Amir Saied Iravani, has said that physical remnants recovered from the weaponry used in the US -Israeli war of aggression against Iran confirm that such weapons formed part of the arsenals of certain Persian Gulf states. He said that those remnants were recovered across multiple affected Iranian cities, adding “the evidence presented clearly demonstrates that those States have not only aided and abetted the aggressors but have themselves engaged in the commission of acts of aggression, as defined under customary international law.”

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian: “The essence of civilizations reveals itself at critical historical junctures. The positions taken by Spain, China, Russia, Türkiye, Italy, and Egypt against the warmongering and crimes of the Zionist regime stem from their deep cultural and historical roots.”

At least two ships sailing from Iranian ports crossed the Strait of Hormuz despite a blockade threat by the US military, maritime tracking reports have indicated. The ships were among at least four Iran-linked vessels that used the route after Washington's threat, according to maritime data provider Kpler. The Liberia-flagged bulk carrier Christianna crossed after unloading 74,000 tons of corn at the Iranian port of Bandar Imam Khomeini, passing Iran's Larak Island in the strait around 1600 GMT on April 13, Kpler data showed. A second ship, the Comoros-flagged tanker Elpis, was near Larak Island around 1100 GMT and cleared the strait about 1600 GMT. It was loaded with 31,000 tons of methanol

🎯DISMANTLED: 4 underground tunnel routes in Gaza, east of the Yellow Line.

The total length of the tunnels were ~800 meters. Within the underground tunnel routes, the troops located living quarters, equipment for prolonged stays, mines, and materials used by Hamas to prepare explosive devices against our troops.
The US and Iran are using new weapons in the war. Here’s what to know.

🔴ELIMINATED: A terrorist cell in Bint Jbeil.

The troops encountered a terrorist cell consisting of three operatives who opened fire at them.

During the incident, three IDF soldiers were severely injured, one was moderately injured, and six additional soldiers were lightly injured.

🔴 In 2 separate instances, IDF troops identified armed Hamas terrorists in the area of the Yellow Line who approached them and posed an immediate threat.

The terrorists were precisely struck and eliminated in order to remove the immediate danger to the troops.

🎯BODY-CAM FOOTAGE: Dozens of Hezbollah terrorist infrastructure sites, including anti-tank positions, observation posts, and weapons storage facilities.

Several weapons belonging to Hezbollah were located inside a civilian home, including Kalashnikov rifles, magazines, and military vests.

🎯⏰24HR RECAP: ~150 Hezbollah targets were struck in numerous areas across southern Lebanon.

Accomplishments:
- Rocket launchers & UAVs struck
- Military structures, anti-tank missile launch points & terror command centers were targeted
- Terrorist cells that attempted to carry out a terror attack against IDF soldiers were thwarted

🔴ELIMINATED: A number of armed terrorists from Hamas who had approached the Yellow Line in central Gaza.

The terrorists posed an immediate threat to IDF troops and were eliminated in a precise strike.

Among the terrorists was Yousef Ibrahim Mahmoud Basheti, a Hamas terrorist who was part of the cell that fired an anti-tank missile in January 2024, killing 21 IDF soldiers.
